# Copilot Instructions

## Project Guidelines
- User prefers clean coding principles: favor single responsibility, early returns, avoid if/else or switch statements where possible, and keep functions under 10 lines where practical.
- Conduct ongoing clean-code audits to keep file size down, ideally under 400 lines, while preserving behavior.
- Provide explanations of changes made, especially for complex software engineering concepts, to aid in learning software engineering. This includes offering clarifications on tricky concepts while implementing features.
- Ensure the how-to-play modal appears while players are loading into the game, not in the lobby before the game starts.
- If a plan is created, continue implementing it in the same response. Do not stop after planning and make the user ask to execute the plan.
